title: Team data model + registry + programming team as instance #1
description: Introduce a `Team` data type and a `loadTeam(config, teamId?)` resolver in a new src/teams/ module, with a built-in 'programming' team that reproduces today's behavior exactly. Add an optional `teams` record and `defaultTeam` field to BoberConfig (Zod). loadTeam(config) with no id returns the programming team whose providers deep-equal resolveRoleProviders(config), whose pipelineShape equals the resolved engine, and whose memoryNamespace maps to the current `.bober/memory/` path. Named teams are built from config entries (providers merged over resolved defaults). This sprint is purely additive resolution + schema: it does NOT yet namespace memory (Sprint 2) or change pipeline-shape selection at runtime (Sprint 3). Zero behavior change is a hard requirement.

successCriteria.description: The project builds successfully with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build).
TypeScript compilation produces zero type errors in strict mode (npm run typecheck).
All tests pass with zero failures, including new collocated tests for src/teams (npm run test).

A Team type and loadTeam(config, teamId?) exist in src/teams/. A unit test asserts loadTeam(config) (no id) returns id 'programming', providers deep-equal resolveRoleProviders(config), pipelineShape equals resolveEngineName(config) (or config.pipeline.engine default 'ts'), and memoryNamespace is the value that maps to the current .bober/memory/ path (e.g. '' or 'programming' sentinel).

BoberConfigSchema (src/config/schema.ts) parses an optional `teams` record (id -> { displayName?, memoryNamespace?, pipelineShape?, providers? (partial role->provider), roles?, guardrails? }) and an optional `defaultTeam` string. A unit test parses a config containing a `teams` entry + `defaultTeam` successfully, and a config WITHOUT either still parses (back-compat).

loadTeam(config, '<id>') for a declared team returns a Team built from that entry: providers from the entry merged over the resolved defaults (resolveRoleProviders), memoryNamespace and pipelineShape from the entry. A unit test verifies a partial provider override (e.g. generator -> deepseek) is applied while unspecified roles keep the resolved default.
loadTeam(config, 'does-not-exist') throws an Error whose message names the missing team id. Covered by a unit test asserting the throw and the id substring.
No @anthropic-ai/sdk or openai SDK import appears anywhere under src/teams (grep returns nothing); provider routing reuses resolveRoleProviders.

nonGoals: Do not namespace the memory store yet — that is Sprint 2 (loadTeam may carry memoryNamespace, but memory.ts is untouched this sprint)
Do not change runtime pipeline-shape selection — that is Sprint 3 (the Team carries pipelineShape, but selectPipelineEngine/runPipeline are untouched this sprint)
Do not add CLI --team flags or a second example team — that is Sprint 4
Do not change the behavior of resolveRoleProviders, selectPipelineEngine, runPipeline, or the memory store
Do not build a .bober/teams/*.json file registry (Option A only)
stopConditions: loadTeam(config) reproduces today's resolved providers + engine + memory path for the programming team, and a config-declared team resolves with its overrides
definitionOfDone: A new src/teams/ module exposes a Team type ({ id, displayName, memoryNamespace, providers: RoleProviderMap, pipelineShape: PipelineEngineName, roles: Role[], guardrails?: unknown }) and loadTeam(config, teamId?): Team. A built-in 'programming' team is registered in code and is the default when no id (or 'programming') is passed; it reproduces resolveRoleProviders(config), the resolved engine, and the current .bober/memory/ path. BoberConfig gains an optional `teams` record + `defaultTeam`, validated by the existing Zod schema, with both absent still valid. Unknown team ids throw a clear error. No existing behavior changes; build, typecheck, and all tests pass; no SDK types leak into src/teams.
assumptions: The memoryNamespace value for the programming/default team is a sentinel ('' or 'programming') that Sprint 2 will map to the existing .bober/memory/ path; this sprint only stores the value.
PipelineEngineName is the existing union from src/orchestrator/workflow/engine.ts ('ts'|'skill'|'workflow'); pipelineShape reuses it.
roles is descriptor metadata (name/displayName) for the 7 existing RoleName values; it does not drive execution in this sprint.
guardrails is a forward-compat optional field, unused and unenforced (medical is Phase 6).
outOfScope: Any change to existing public behavior
generatorNotes:
Create src/teams/ with small single-purpose files (principle: small modules, unicode box-header section comments), e.g. src/teams/types.ts (Team, Role) and src/teams/registry.ts (builtin programming team + loadTeam). Collocate *.test.ts.

Verified reuse signatures:
- resolveRoleProviders(config: BoberConfig): RoleProviderMap — src/config/role-providers.ts:97. RoleName union ('planner'|'researcher'|'curator'|'generator'|'evaluator'|'codeReview'|'chat') at :10; ALL_ROLES order at :38. RoleProviderMap = Record<RoleName,string> at :19.
- resolveEngineName(config): PipelineEngineName — src/orchestrator/workflow/selector.ts:22 (pure, returns the name without instantiating). PipelineEngineName/PipelineEngine — src/orchestrator/workflow/engine.ts. config.pipeline.engine defaults to 'ts' (createDefaultConfig in src/config/schema.ts).
- BoberConfigSchema — src/config/schema.ts (full config object ~line 361). Add at the end: teams: z.record(z.string(), TeamConfigSchema).optional(), defaultTeam: z.string().optional(). Define TeamConfigSchema with all-optional fields: displayName?, memoryNamespace? (z.string().regex to safe segment), pipelineShape? (reuse the engine enum), providers? (z.record over RoleName -> string, partial), roles? , guardrails? (z.unknown()). Keep createDefaultConfig emitting NO teams (back-compat).

loadTeam(config, teamId?):
- If teamId is undefined or 'programming' (or === config.defaultTeam when that points to the builtin): return the builtin programming team: { id:'programming', displayName:'Programming team', memoryNamespace:'' (sentinel for current path), providers: resolveRoleProviders(config), pipelineShape: resolveEngineName(config), roles: the 7 roles as descriptors, guardrails: undefined }.
- Else if config.teams?.[teamId] exists: build a Team from it. providers = { ...resolveRoleProviders(config), ...(entry.providers ?? {}) } so unspecified roles keep resolved defaults. memoryNamespace = entry.memoryNamespace ?? teamId. pipelineShape = entry.pipelineShape ?? resolveEngineName(config). roles = entry.roles ?? default descriptors. guardrails = entry.guardrails.
- Else: throw new Error(`Unknown team '${teamId}'. Declare it under config.teams or use the built-in 'programming' team.`).

Do NOT wire loadTeam into runPipeline/chat behavior this sprint beyond what is needed to keep things compiling — the goal is the data model + resolver + schema, fully unit-tested, with zero behavior change. If you add a thin call site, ensure the default path is exactly today's behavior.

Tests: construct a BoberConfig via createDefaultConfig (or a fixture) and assert loadTeam(config) deep-equals the expected programming team fields. For sc-1-6 declare a teams entry with providers:{generator:'deepseek'} and assert the merge. For sc-1-7 assert the throw. Use no network and no fs (pure functions).
evaluatorNotes:
sc-1-4: call loadTeam(config) (no id); assert id==='programming', deepEqual(team.providers, resolveRoleProviders(config)), team.pipelineShape===resolveEngineName(config), and team.memoryNamespace is the documented sentinel.
sc-1-5: parse via BoberConfigSchema a config object with teams:{docs:{memoryNamespace:'docs',pipelineShape:'ts',providers:{generator:'deepseek'}}} and defaultTeam:'programming' — expect success; parse a config WITHOUT teams/defaultTeam — expect success.
sc-1-6: loadTeam(config,'docs') -> providers.generator==='deepseek' AND providers.planner===resolveRoleProviders(config).planner (unspecified role unchanged); memoryNamespace==='docs'.
sc-1-7: expect loadTeam(config,'nope') to throw with message containing 'nope'.
sc-1-8: grep -r '@anthropic-ai/sdk\|from "openai"' src/teams — expect no matches.
Regression: run the FULL suite (npm run test) and confirm nothing else changed (programming default behavior intact). Run npm run build and npm run typecheck.
